title: erythroxylum areolatum
intro: Erythroxylum areolatum, also known as the Puna Kokab, is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae. It is a shrub-like plant native to the Andean region, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of skin conditions. Erythroxylum areolatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for use in herbal medicine, and its extracts are also used in the production of traditional remedies.
